children.name: Opisthocomus
children.description: A monotypic genus and one of the most bizarre and ancient bird lineages alive today. The Hoatzin is a unique evolutionary marvel, possessing a foregut fermentation digestive system similar to that of ruminant mammals — a trait found in no other bird. Its chicks have functional claws on their wings used for climbing through vegetation, a feature that reverts in adulthood.

children.name: Opisthocomus hoazin
children.description: A bizarre, pheasant-sized bird found in the flooded forests and oxbow lakes of the Amazon and Orinoco river basins. It has a spiky crest, bare blue face, and pungent odour caused by its unique digestive process — it ferments leaves in its foregut like a cow, earning it the local name 'stinking bird'. Hoatzin chicks retain the ancestral reptilian trait of wing claws, which they use to clamber through branches until they can fly.
